symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic tachycardia with gray-out settling supine, plus diffuse joint pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural tachycardia/gray-out settling supine with hypermobility-related pain fits POTS secondary to hEDS.
Strictly postural tachycardia that settles supine is against a primary arrhythmia, but ECG is needed.
No bleeding and normal supine BP lower occult-bleed probability, though FBC helps.
No deformed/stuck joint and intact neurovascular status argue against an acute dislocation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true syncope/chest pain; racing settles supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No true LOC/chest pain and supine settling are reassuring, though ECG is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Very high fall risk (joint instability + orthostasis), isolated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Combined hypermobility and orthostatic gray-out while alone create a very high fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural HR 80->138 with borderline hypotension; Beighton 7/9; joints located; diffuse myofascial tenderness (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A >30 bpm postural rise with a high Beighton score and located joints fits POTS secondary to hEDS.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Postural tachycardia/gray-out settling supine with hypermobility-related diffuse pain, no bleeding and no current dislocation, is a POTS/hEDS flare - but arrhythmia/occult bleed/dislocation can't be excluded remotely and the fall risk is very high, so urgent in-person review (ECG + joints) plus fall-safety and supportive measures (binder, salt/fluids, bracing) is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude arrhythmia and document the postural response.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person joint examination (± FBC if bleeding concern)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess hypermobility/joints and exclude bleeding contributors.
To support pooling and joint stability.
